summary: High‑energy Rochester audience tape with crystal‑clear sound captures a smoking Sugaree, epic IKO→Morning Dew and jam‑filled second set.
review: This audience recording delivers surprisingly clear and dynamic audio that lets the band's 1980‑era power shine through. The performance is electric – standout moments include a scorching Sugaree, tight China Cat/Rider, a monster Estimated>Terrapin>Playing sequence and an unforgettable IKO into Morning Dew. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent’s keyboards sparkle, and the rhythm section drives the whole night with thunder‑filled grooves.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 enthusiastic reviews (mostly 5 stars) praise both the musical performance and the surprisingly good audience recording; weighting favors the show quality, yielding a near‑top rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally praised as clear, dynamic and well‑balanced for an AUD; early copies were muddy but the archived version is considered one of the best sounding audience tapes of this show.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set builds into a jam‑heavy second set with seamless transitions; the band stays on the groove from start to finish, delivering multiple epic jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his soaring guitar tone, powerful solos and even vocal teasing (e.g., Scarlet Begonias riff).
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is described as “thunder juice” and nicely up‑front in the mix on several jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s Rhodes/tiny piano work gets special praise – “dynoed‑Rhodes tone,” “toy piano solos” are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey are lauded for a thunderous, driving rhythm that fuels the jams.
